name: Inverted Row
cues: Pull the chest to the bar
Squeeze the shoulder blades, control down
execution_steps: Set a bar at about hip height in a rack or Smith machine.
Lie underneath and grip slightly wider than shoulder-width, arms extended.
Set a straight line from heels to head, bracing the glutes and core.
Pull your chest to the bar, driving the elbows down and squeezing the shoulder blades.
Lower under control to full arm extension, keeping the hips up.
good_when: You train with minimal equipment or bodyweight and want a horizontal pull.
You're a beginner building back strength and scapular control before loaded rows.
bad_when: You need heavy progressive overload and the hardest bodyweight variation is too easy (use barbell or cable rows).
You can't yet hold a rigid body line and your hips sag (regress to a more upright angle first).
